testcase.name: test_mixed_case_local_part_still_sends

failure.message: AssertionError: forgot-password did not normalize the email local-part (W1) assert 'user.password_reset_requested' in [] + where [] = audit_actions()

testcase.name: test_login_admin

failure.message: Failed: cannot create admin via ORM (enum mismatch, BUG-AUTH-001): DataError

testcase.failure:
self = <sqlalchemy.engine.base.Connection object at 0x00000170A7110EF0> dialect = <sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql.psycopg.PGDialect_psycopg object at 0x0000017083AE76B0> context = <sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql.psycopg.PGExecutionContext_psycopg object at 0x00000170A7246600> statement = <sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql.psycopg.PGCompiler_psycopg object at 0x00000170A7247E30> parameters = [{'id': UUID('6a5073cd-901d-4aaf-93b6-c39041b96d91'), 'email': 'admin@qatest.io', 'password_hash': '$2b$12$tonFkqoc.6uPGvh2QYt0..STHBC.Ki0jadew2IdNKQeX3VmFV0Xnq', 'role': 'ADMIN', ...}]
def _exec_single_context( self, dialect: Dialect, context: ExecutionContext, statement: Union[str, Compiled], parameters: Optional[_AnyMultiExecuteParams], ) -> CursorResult[Any]: """continue the _execute_context() method for a single DBAPI cursor.execute() or cursor.executemany() call.
""" if dialect.bind_typing is BindTyping.SETINPUTSIZES: generic_setinputsizes = context._prepare_set_input_sizes()
if generic_setinputsizes: try: dialect.do_set_input_sizes( context.cursor, generic_setinputsizes, context ) except BaseException as e: self._handle_dbapi_exception( e, str(statement), parameters, None, context )
cursor, str_statement, parameters = ( context.cursor, context.statement, context.parameters, )
effective_parameters: Optional[_AnyExecuteParams]
if not context.executemany: effective_parameters = parameters[0] else: effective_parameters = parameters
if self._has_events or self.engine._has_events: for fn in self.dispatch.before_cursor_execute: str_statement, effective_parameters = fn( self, cursor, str_statement, effective_parameters, context, context.executemany, )
if self._echo: self._log_info(str_statement)
stats = context._get_cache_stats()
if not self.engine.hide_parameters: self._log_info( "[%s] %r", stats, sql_util._repr_params( effective_parameters, batches=10, ismulti=context.executemany, ), ) else: self._log_info( "[%s] [SQL parameters hidden due to hide_parameters=True]", stats, )
evt_handled: bool = False try: if context.execute_style is ExecuteStyle.EXECUTEMANY: effective_parameters = cast( "_CoreMultiExecuteParams", effective_parameters ) if self.dialect._has_events: for fn in self.dialect.dispatch.do_executemany: if fn( cursor, str_statement, effective_parameters, context, ): evt_handled = True break if not evt_handled: self.dialect.do_executemany( cursor, str_statement, effective_parameters, context, ) elif not effective_parameters and context.no_parameters: if self.dialect._has_events: for fn in self.dialect.dispatch.do_execute_no_params: if fn(cursor, str_statement, context): evt_handled = True break if not evt_handled: self.dialect.do_execute_no_params( cursor, str_statement, context ) else: effective_parameters = cast( "_CoreSingleExecuteParams", effective_parameters ) if self.dialect._has_events: for fn in self.dialect.dispatch.do_execute: if fn( cursor, str_statement, effective_parameters, context, ): evt_handled = True break if not evt_handled: > self.dialect.do_execute( cursor, str_statement, effective_parameters, context )

self = <psycopg.Cursor [closed] [IDLE] (host=localhost port=5433 user=trading database=trading_app_test) at 0x170a6fe3050> query = 'INSERT INTO users (id, email, password_hash, role, display_name, business_name, is_active, email_verified, email_veri...e)s, %(email_verified)s, %(email_verified_at)s::TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) RETURNING users.created_at, users.updated_at' params = {'id': UUID('6a5073cd-901d-4aaf-93b6-c39041b96d91'), 'email': 'admin@qatest.io', 'password_hash': '$2b$12$tonFkqoc.6uPGvh2QYt0..STHBC.Ki0jadew2IdNKQeX3VmFV0Xnq', 'role': 'ADMIN', ...} prepare = None, binary = None
def execute( self, query: Query, params: Params | None = None, *, prepare: bool | None = None, binary: bool | None = None, ) -> Self: """ Execute a query or command to the database. """ try: with self._conn.lock: self._conn.wait( self._execute_gen(query, params, prepare=prepare, binary=binary) ) except e._NO_TRACEBACK as ex: > raise ex.with_traceback(None) E psycopg.errors.InvalidTextRepresentation: invalid input value for enum user_role: "ADMIN" E CONTEXT: unnamed portal parameter $4 = '...'
..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\psycopg\cursor.py:97: InvalidTextRepresentation
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
client = <starlette.testclient.TestClient object at 0x00000170A6FF5100> db = <sqlalchemy.orm.session.Session object at 0x00000170A7110440>
@pytest.mark.candidate_bug def test_login_admin(client, db): # AUTH-LOGIN-FUNC-003 / BUG-AUTH-001 """Admin login requires an admin user. The user_role enum stores 'admin' lowercase while TRADER/SUBSCRIBER are stored as their uppercase member names, so the ORM can neither create nor read an admin row. This SHOULD succeed once the enum label is fixed.""" try: u = User(email="admin@qatest.io", password_hash=hash_password(DEFAULT_PW), role=UserRole.ADMIN, is_active=True) db.add(u) > db.commit()
tests\test_auth_login.py:47: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\sqlalchemy\orm\session.py:2028: in commit trans.commit(_to_root=True) <string>:2: in commit ??? ..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\sqlalchemy\orm\state_changes.py:139: in _go ret_value = fn(self, *arg, **kw) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ ..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\sqlalchemy\orm\session.py:1313: in commit self._prepare_impl() <string>:2: in _prepare_impl ??? ..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\sqlalchemy\orm\state_changes.py:139: in _go ret_value = fn(self, *arg, **kw) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ ..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\sqlalchemy\orm\session.py:1288: in _prepare_impl self.session.flush() util.preloaded.orm_persistence.save_obj( ..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\sqlalchemy\orm\persistence.py:93: in save_obj _emit_insert_statements( ..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\sqlalchemy\orm\persistence.py:1233: in _emit_insert_statements result = connection.execute( ..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\sqlalchemy\engine\base.py:1418: in execute return meth( ..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\sqlalchemy\sql\elements.py:515: in _execute_on_connection return connection._execute_clauseelement( ..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\sqlalchemy\engine\base.py:1640: in ..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\sqlalchemy\engine\base.py:1986: in _exec_single_context self._handle_dbapi_exception( ..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\sqlalchemy\engine\base.py:2355: in _handle_dbapi_exception raise sqlalchemy_exception.with_traceback(exc_info[2]) from e ..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\sqlalchemy\engine\base.py:1967: in _exec_single_context self.dialect.do_execute( ..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\sqlalchemy\engine\default.py:941: in do_execute cursor.execute(statement, parameters) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <psycopg.Cursor [closed] [IDLE] (host=localhost port=5433 user=trading database=trading_app_test) at 0x170a6fe3050> query = 'INSERT INTO users (id, email, password_hash, role, display_name, business_name, is_active, email_verified, email_veri...e)s, %(email_verified)s, %(email_verified_at)s::TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) RETURNING users.created_at, users.updated_at' params = {'id': UUID('6a5073cd-901d-4aaf-93b6-c39041b96d91'), 'email': 'admin@qatest.io', 'password_hash': '$2b$12$tonFkqoc.6uPGvh2QYt0..STHBC.Ki0jadew2IdNKQeX3VmFV0Xnq', 'role': 'ADMIN', ...} prepare = None, binary = None
def execute( self, query: Query, params: Params | None = None, *, prepare: bool | None = None, binary: bool | None = None, ) -> Self: """ Execute a query or command to the database. """ try: with self._conn.lock: self._conn.wait( self._execute_gen(query, params, prepare=prepare, binary=binary) ) except e._NO_TRACEBACK as ex: > raise ex.with_traceback(None) E sqlalchemy.exc.DataError: (psycopg.errors.InvalidTextRepresentation) invalid input value for enum user_role: "ADMIN" E CONTEXT: unnamed portal parameter $4 = '...' E [SQL: INSERT INTO users (id, email, password_hash, role, display_name, business_name, is_active, email_verified, email_verified_at) VALUES (%(id)s::UUID, %(email)s::VARCHAR, %(password_hash)s::VARCHAR, %(role)s, %(display_name)s::VARCHAR, %(business_name)s::VARCHAR, %(is_active)s, %(email_verified)s, %(email_verified_at)s::TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) RETURNING users.created_at, users.updated_at] E [parameters: {'id': UUID('6a5073cd-901d-4aaf-93b6-c39041b96d91'), 'email': 'admin@qatest.io', 'password_hash': '$2b$12$tonFkqoc.6uPGvh2QYt0..STHBC.Ki0jadew2IdNKQeX3VmFV0Xnq', 'role': 'ADMIN', 'display_name': None, 'business_name': None, 'is_active': True, 'email_verified': False, 'email_verified_at': None}] E (Background on this error at: https://sqlalche.me/e/20/9h9h)
..\.venv\Lib\site-packages\psycopg\cursor.py:97: DataError
During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:
client = <starlette.testclient.TestClient object at 0x00000170A6FF5100> db = <sqlalchemy.orm.session.Session object at 0x00000170A7110440>
@pytest.mark.candidate_bug def test_login_admin(client, db): # AUTH-LOGIN-FUNC-003 / BUG-AUTH-001 """Admin login requires an admin user. The user_role enum stores 'admin' lowercase while TRADER/SUBSCRIBER are stored as their uppercase member names, so the ORM can neither create nor read an admin row. This SHOULD succeed once the enum label is fixed.""" try: u = User(email="admin@qatest.io", password_hash=hash_password(DEFAULT_PW), role=UserRole.ADMIN, is_active=True) db.add(u) db.commit() except Exception as e: # noqa: BLE001 db.rollback() > pytest.fail(f"cannot create admin via ORM (enum mismatch, BUG-AUTH-001): {type(e).__name__}") E Failed: cannot create admin via ORM (enum mismatch, BUG-AUTH-001): DataError
tests\test_auth_login.py:50: Failed

testcase.name: test_reset_enforces_complexity

failure.message: AssertionError: reset-password accepted a password register would reject (W2) assert 200 == 422 + where 200 = <Response [200 OK]>.status_code

testcase.name: test_reset_caps_at_72_bytes

failure.message: AssertionError: reset-password accepts >72-byte password (bcrypt truncates) (W2) assert 200 == 422 + where 200 = <Response [200 OK]>.status_code
